lines.english: Lesson 21
lines.chinese: 第21课
lines.raw: Lesson 21 | 第21课

lines.english: Daniel Mendoza
lines.chinese: 丹尼尔·门多萨
lines.raw: Daniel Mendoza | 丹尼尔·门多萨

lines.english: How many unsuccessful attempts did Mendoza make before becoming Champion of all England?
lines.chinese: 门多萨在成为全英冠军之前，有过多少次失败的尝试？
lines.raw: How many unsuccessful attempts did Mendoza make before becoming Champion of all England? | 门多萨在成为全英冠军之前，有过多少次失败的尝试？

lines.english: Boxing matches were very popular in England two hundred years ago.
lines.chinese: 两百年前，拳击比赛在英格兰非常盛行。
lines.raw: Boxing matches were very popular in England two hundred years ago. | 两百年前，拳击比赛在英格兰非常盛行。

lines.english: In those days, boxers fought with bare fists for prize money.
lines.chinese: 在那些日子里，拳击手们赤手空拳地搏斗以争夺奖金。
lines.raw: In those days, boxers fought with bare fists for prize money. | 在那些日子里，拳击手们赤手空拳地搏斗以争夺奖金。

lines.english: Because of this, they were known as 'prizefighters'.
lines.chinese: 正因为如此，他们被称为“职业拳击手”。
lines.raw: Because of this, they were known as 'prizefighters'. | 正因为如此，他们被称为“职业拳击手”。

lines.english: However, boxing was very crude,
lines.chinese: 然而，拳击是非常原始的，
lines.raw: However, boxing was very crude, | 然而，拳击是非常原始的，

lines.english: for there were no rules and a prizefighter could be seriously injured or even killed during a match.
lines.chinese: 因为当时没有规则，职业拳击手在比赛中可能会受重伤，甚至死亡。
lines.raw: for there were no rules and a prizefighter could be seriously injured or even killed during a match. | 因为当时没有规则，职业拳击手在比赛中可能会受重伤，甚至死亡。

lines.english: One of the most colourful figures in boxing history was Daniel Mendoza, who was born in 1764.
lines.chinese: 拳击史上最引人注目的人物之一是丹尼尔·门多萨，他出生于1764年。
lines.raw: One of the most colourful figures in boxing history was Daniel Mendoza, who was born in 1764. | 拳击史上最引人注目的人物之一是丹尼尔·门多萨，他出生于1764年。

lines.english: The use of gloves was not introduced until 1860, when the Marquis of Queensberry drew up the first set of rules.
lines.chinese: 直到1860年，昆斯伯里侯爵制定了第一套规则后，才引入了手套的使用。
lines.raw: The use of gloves was not introduced until 1860, when the Marquis of Queensberry drew up the first set of rules. | 直到1860年，昆斯伯里侯爵制定了第一套规则后，才引入了手套的使用。

lines.english: Though he was technically a prizefighter,
lines.chinese: 尽管从技术上讲他是一名职业拳击手，
lines.raw: Though he was technically a prizefighter, | 尽管从技术上讲他是一名职业拳击手，

lines.english: Mendoza did much to change crude prizefighting into a sport, for he brought science to the game.
lines.chinese: 但门多萨为将原始的职业拳击比赛转变为一项运动做了很多工作，因为他将科学带入了这项运动。
lines.raw: Mendoza did much to change crude prizefighting into a sport, for he brought science to the game. | 但门多萨为将原始的职业拳击比赛转变为一项运动做了很多工作，因为他将科学带入了这项运动。

lines.english: In his day, Mendoza enjoyed tremendous popularity.
lines.chinese: 在他那个时代，门多萨享有巨大的声望。
lines.raw: In his day, Mendoza enjoyed tremendous popularity. | 在他那个时代，门多萨享有巨大的声望。

lines.english: He was adored by rich and poor alike.
lines.chinese: 他深受富人和穷人的喜爱。
lines.raw: He was adored by rich and poor alike. | 他深受富人和穷人的喜爱。

lines.english: Mendoza rose to fame swiftly after a boxing match when he was only fourteen years old.
lines.chinese: 门多萨在14岁时参加一场拳击比赛后迅速成名。
lines.raw: Mendoza rose to fame swiftly after a boxing match when he was only fourteen years old. | 门多萨在14岁时参加一场拳击比赛后迅速成名。

lines.english: This attracted the attention of Richard Humphries who was then the most eminent boxer in England.
lines.chinese: 这引起了理查德·汉弗莱斯的注意，他当时是英格兰最杰出的拳击手。
lines.raw: This attracted the attention of Richard Humphries who was then the most eminent boxer in England. | 这引起了理查德·汉弗莱斯的注意，他当时是英格兰最杰出的拳击手。

lines.english: He offered to train Mendoza and his young pupil was quick to learn.
lines.chinese: 他主动提出训练门多萨，而他的年轻学生也学得很快。
lines.raw: He offered to train Mendoza and his young pupil was quick to learn. | 他主动提出训练门多萨，而他的年轻学生也学得很快。

lines.english: In fact, Mendoza soon became so successful that Humphries turned against him.
lines.chinese: 事实上，门多萨很快就变得非常成功，以至于汉弗莱斯转而反对他。
lines.raw: In fact, Mendoza soon became so successful that Humphries turned against him. | 事实上，门多萨很快就变得非常成功，以至于汉弗莱斯转而反对他。

lines.english: The two men quarrelled bitterly and it was clear that the argument could only be settled by a fight.
lines.chinese: 两人激烈争吵，很明显，争论只能通过一场战斗来解决。
lines.raw: The two men quarrelled bitterly and it was clear that the argument could only be settled by a fight. | 两人激烈争吵，很明显，争论只能通过一场战斗来解决。

lines.english: A match was held at Stilton, where both men fought for an hour.
lines.chinese: 在斯蒂尔顿举行了一场比赛，两人激战了一个小时。
lines.raw: A match was held at Stilton, where both men fought for an hour. | 在斯蒂尔顿举行了一场比赛，两人激战了一个小时。

lines.english: The public bet a great deal of money on Mendoza, but he was defeated.
lines.chinese: 公众在门多萨身上下了大笔赌注，但他被击败了。
lines.raw: The public bet a great deal of money on Mendoza, but he was defeated. | 公众在门多萨身上下了大笔赌注，但他被击败了。

lines.english: Mendoza met Humphries in the ring on a later occasion and he lost for a second time.
lines.chinese: 门多萨后来又在拳击台上与汉弗莱斯相遇，但他第二次输了。
lines.raw: Mendoza met Humphries in the ring on a later occasion and he lost for a second time. | 门多萨后来又在拳击台上与汉弗莱斯相遇，但他第二次输了。

lines.english: It was not until his third match in 1790 that he finally beat Humphries and became Champion of England.
lines.chinese: 直到1790年的第三场比赛，他才最终击败汉弗莱斯，成为英格兰冠军。
lines.raw: It was not until his third match in 1790 that he finally beat Humphries and became Champion of England. | 直到1790年的第三场比赛，他才最终击败汉弗莱斯，成为英格兰冠军。

lines.english: Meanwhile, he founded a highly successful Academy and even Lord Byron became one of his pupils.
lines.chinese: 同时，他创办了一所非常成功的拳击学院，甚至拜伦勋爵也成了他的学生之一。
lines.raw: Meanwhile, he founded a highly successful Academy and even Lord Byron became one of his pupils. | 同时，他创办了一所非常成功的拳击学院，甚至拜伦勋爵也成了他的学生之一。

lines.english: He earned enormous sums of money and was paid as much as ￡100 for a single appearance.
lines.chinese: 他赚取了巨额财富，单次出场费高达100英镑。
lines.raw: He earned enormous sums of money and was paid as much as ￡100 for a single appearance. | 他赚取了巨额财富，单次出场费高达100英镑。

lines.english: Despite this, he was so extravagant that he was always in debt.
lines.chinese: 尽管如此，他非常奢侈，总是负债累累。
lines.raw: Despite this, he was so extravagant that he was always in debt. | 尽管如此，他非常奢侈，总是负债累累。

lines.english: After he was defeated by a boxer called Gentleman Jackson, he was quickly forgotten.
lines.chinese: 在他被一位名叫“绅士”杰克逊的拳击手击败后，他很快就被遗忘了。
lines.raw: After he was defeated by a boxer called Gentleman Jackson, he was quickly forgotten. | 在他被一位名叫“绅士”杰克逊的拳击手击败后，他很快就被遗忘了。

lines.english: He was sent to prison for failing to pay his debts and died in poverty in 1836.
lines.chinese: 他因未能偿还债务而被送进监狱，并于1836年贫困潦倒地去世。
lines.raw: He was sent to prison for failing to pay his debts and died in poverty in 1836. | 他因未能偿还债务而被送进监狱，并于1836年贫困潦倒地去世。
